summary: mailconfirmer postinstall drops and executes a remote Windows binary, then erases its own traces
details: On `npm install`, the postinstall hook `install-hook.js` launches a hidden PowerShell process via `powershell -NoP -NonI -W Hidden -Enc <base64>`. The decoded payload downloads `https://github.com/Dimitrijenco/Sticky_note/releases/download/v2/launcher.bin`, XOR-decrypts the bytes with key 0x42, writes the result to `%TEMP%\tmp.exe`, runs it with `Start-Process -WindowStyle Hidden`, and deletes the EXE. A detached Node cleanup script written to `%TEMP%/cleanup_<ts>.js` then edits the installer's project `package.json` and `package-lock.json` to remove the `mailconfirmer` entries and `fs.rmSync`'s the module directory under `node_modules/`, hiding evidence that the package was ever installed. The package's advertised `main` (`index.js`) only ships stub `sendConfirmationEmail` / `verifyToken` functions that `console.log` and resolve — no real email functionality exists; the email-confirmation description is a cover story for the dropper.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
